explanations.1: PWD:=$(shell pwd) captures the current working directory by running the shell command pwd and storing it in a variable named PWD that can be used throughout the Makefile.
explanations.3: current_dir is a Makefile target (like a function name) that defines a task you can run by typing 'make current_dir'.
explanations.4: The @ symbol tells Make to run this echo command silently without printing the command itself, and ${PWD} substitutes the directory value we captured earlier.
